From Kipchak *ülkä (“administrative region”), from Proto-Turkic *ül- (“to divide”).
Cognate with Old Turkic [script needed] (ülkä, “administrative region”);
Kazakh өлке (ölke), Bashkir өлкә (ölkə, “region”), Uzbek oʻlka,
Uyghur ئولكە (olke, “country; province”),
Turkish ülke (“country”), Azerbaijani ölkə (“country”), etc.
